geopolitics_summary: Middle East tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruption risk underpin energy security concerns; EU growth forecast cut; Russia sanctions policy in flux.

themes.headline: Europe faces record May heatwave; climate agency warns intensification trend
themes.why_it_matters: Earlier/deeper peak-summer cooling demand stress test. Elevated real-time balancing risk and potential for hydro constraints in Alpine regions if heatwave persists into June. DA spreads and intraday volatility likely higher.

themes.headline: EU growth forecasts cut as Middle East war continues; energy prices cited
themes.why_it_matters: EC explicitly links energy-price inflation to growth slowdown. Sustained Hormuz disruption risk elevates LNG and crude premia, raising EU power curve via gas-to-power switching and industrial fuel mix pressure.

themes.headline: Sweden open to NATO role in Strait of Hormuz security restoration
themes.why_it_matters: Military intervention signal could reduce Hormuz closure risk premium embedded in LNG spot/forward curves and Brent. Lower LNG costs weaken TTF, pressuring EU gas and marginal-gen power spreads.

themes.headline: EU ministers debate energy security amid Strait of Hormuz crisis backdrop
themes.why_it_matters: High-level EU energy security review signals potential policy tightening (storage mandates, LNG contracts, interconnects). Direct driver of medium-term power curve risk via fuel supply strategies and commodity reserves.

themes.headline: U.S. industrial natural gas consumption forecast to hit record highs 2026–2027
themes.why_it_matters: Peak US nat-gas industrial demand reduces LNG export availability for NW Europe, tightening global LNG arb and supporting TTF via constrained spot imports during winter ramp.

watchlist: Strait of Hormuz disruption escalation or de-escalation; NATO meeting outcomes Friday.
EU energy policy response announcements from Brussels ministerial debate (weeks).
Alpine/Central European hydro conditions through June if heatwave persists.
UK Russia sanctions rollback progress; EU response and sentiment impact on energy diplomacy.
